steps.title: Spot what the phrase requires
steps.content:
The blank must connect “an important [word]” with “Hawai'i Register of Historic Places.” The phrase contains a general noun (list) followed by a specific proper noun (the official name of the register).

These two nouns need punctuation to show how they relate.

steps.title: Apply the apposition rule
steps.content:
A colon introduces a noun phrase that specifies or renames a preceding noun: “an important list: the Hawai'i Register of Historic Places,” where “the Hawai'i Register” clarifies which list is meant.

The other choices either break the sentence into fragments (period in B), misuse punctuation meant for independent clauses (semicolon in C), or leave nouns disconnected without punctuation (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
